soft_violations.note: started_at == finished_at == 2026-04-22T15:00:00Z in run_summary.json. Gemini emits the same timestamp for both endpoints. Not a hard violation — no AUDIT.md rule mandates monotonic separation; the spec only requires both fields present and ISO-8601. Flagged as soft observation for portability tracking, not a fail.

verification.id_format: AUD-YYYY-MM-DD-<TYPE3>-<NNNN> (4-segment date-embedded; matches existing baselined runs and AUDIT.md spec)
verification.index_json_root: bare array (matches existing baselined runs and AUDIT.md exemplar)

per_level_details_schema.epic: what + why only — matches AUDIT.md line 193
per_level_details_schema.story: what + why + (who | when | where) — matches spec
per_level_details_schema.task: all 9 keys including constraints, 5m, cost — matches spec

verification.redaction: applied on src/config.js evidence as [REDACTED:stripe-key]; literal API key value not echoed
verification.hierarchy_chain: epic AUD-2026-04-22-SEC-0001 → story AUD-2026-04-22-SEC-0002 → task AUD-2026-04-22-SEC-0003 (verified)
verification.state_daily_id_parity: all 6 state IDs present in daily mirror; criteria §2 satisfied
verification.expected_findings_match: 1 SEC (hard-coded API key) + 1 PRF (O(n^2) loop) — fixture.yaml expectations met

notes: Manual Antigravity run, Gemini 3.1 Pro (High), temp=0.0, seed=42. First attempt delegated to a Python script (generate.py, 496 lines) and self-reported PASS — rejected by reviewer because that tests the script's portability, not the model's. Re-prompted: 'You ARE the audit agent. Read repo/ files yourself, classify each finding, then write the .audit/ tree files directly using your file tools.' Second attempt produced 9 real artifacts via file tools; +2 more (run_summary.json, banner.txt) after explicit follow-up. Final tree is conformant to AUDIT.md sha256:8d79bb69. No hard violations; 1 soft observation (stylized timestamps).
